mnemonics.title: String Immutability Rule
mnemonics.content: Strings Can't Change, Only Link New Copies (SCCOLNC) - Every modification creates a new string object due to immutability

mnemonics.title: Pool Management
mnemonics.content: Pool Literals, Create New When Changed (PLCNWC) - Identical literals share pool memory until modified

mnemonics.title: Case Sensitivity
mnemonics.content: Case Matters: Upper vs Lower in Comparisons (CMULC) - Remember equals() vs equalsIgnoreCase() difference

mnemonics.title: Conversion Priority
mnemonics.content: Use valueOf() for Versatile Object Conversions (UVOC) - Converts primitives/objects to strings efficiently

acronyms.usage: Remember how string pool optimizes memory for literals

acronyms.expansion: Creating New Objects Combining All Text
acronyms.usage: How + operator creates new strings in background

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a shared library - identical books (strings) are stored once and borrowed by multiple readers (variables)
Picture stone tablets - once carved (created), text can't be changed, only new tablets can be made
Visualize a factory assembly line creating new products (strings) for every + operation
See a toggle switch flipping characters between uppercase (ON) and lowercase (OFF) states

analogies.analogy: Like building a brick wall with sticky notes - each iteration creates a new wall (string) instead of modifying existing one
A car dealership's lot - same model cars (string literals) are shared between customers (variables) to save space
A universal adapter plug that converts any data type (primitives/objects) to standard string format
